title: sauvetrea napoensis
side_effects: Sauvetrea napoensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and allergic reactions. Ingesting the plant can lead to stomach cramps, diarrhea, and vomiting due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or extract can cause skin irritation, rashes, and blistering. Improper use of Sauvetrea napoensis can also trigger respiratory issues, such as wheezing and shortness of breath, especially in individuals with pre-existing conditions.
